title: Binomial distribution
short: The distribution of the number of successes in n independent trials, each with success probability p.
full:
The binomial distribution models the number of successes X in n independent trials when each trial succeeds with the same probability p (a Bernoulli trial). The probability of exactly k successes is P(X=k) = nCk·p^k·(1−p)^(n−k).

Its mean is n·p and its standard deviation is √(n·p·(1−p)). It applies whenever trials are independent, fixed in number, and have two outcomes — like coin flips, multiple-choice guesses, or defective-item counts.
